events.title: Weather tracker: hail covers parts of Tunisia and Algeria like snow
events.summary:
Accumulations of up to 3cm deep reported as severe thunderstorms also bring heavy downpours to central Italy

Severe thunderstorms have affected the Mediterranean this week. On Monday, a surface low-pressure system in the Mediterranean in conjunction with an upper air cut-off low, led to thunderstorms over north Africa. Their intensity was aided by the hot precursor conditions.

Algeria and Tunisia were notably affected by the thunderstorms, with some hail accumulation layers as a result. When so much hail forms, it starts to lay down sheets of hail, covering the ground like snow. Hail accumulations of up to 3cm were reported in Oum Ladjoul and Hammam Sokhna in Algeria, and there were hailstones of up to 3cm in diameter in Makthar, Tunisia. Thunderstorms continued in the region through the following day, with further hail accumulations, notably in Ouled Bousmir, Tunisia, where there was a layer about 2cm deep.

FC Basel and Polish stadium stop US rapper’s upcoming shows, after similar cancellations in France and UK over antisemitic comments

Kanye West’s upcoming concerts in Poland and Switzerland have been cancelled, as a growing number of European countries have stopped or postponed the US rapper’s performances amid a furore over his past antisemitic comments.

Swiss football club FC Basel, which is responsible for concerts and events that take place at its St Jakob-Park ground, told Reuters on Saturday that after reviewing a request for West to perform there in June, it decided against it.

Continue reading...

events.title: Middle East crisis live: Iran sends mixed signals on talks after US seizes ship
events.summary:
Official tells Reuters it is ‘positively reviewing’ involvement in negotiations after earlier saying it had no plans for a new round of talks

The US has just released some more footage of the encounter with the Iranian flagged vessel, the M/V Touska.

In a post on X, US Central Command said US Marines had departed the amphibious assault ship USS Tripoli by helicopter and rappelled onto the Iranian-flagged vessel.

Continue reading...

events.title: JD Vance to lead US delegation in Pakistan if Iran agrees to talks
events.summary:
US vice-president to travel to Islamabad with Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner as deadline for current ceasefire looms

  • Pakistan seeks to raise its global standing in push for Middle East peace

  • Middle East crisis – live updates

JD Vance is expected to fly to Islamabad at the head of a US diplomatic delegation on Tuesday if Iran agrees to further talks in the Pakistani capital as the deadline for the current ceasefire looms.

The US vice-president will travel with Steve Witkoff, Donald Trump’s special envoy, and Jared Kushner, the president’s son-in-law – though Iran’s president warned there remained a “deep historical mistrust” of the US.

Continue reading...

events.title: Israeli army to launch criminal investigation after soldier strikes Jesus statue in Lebanon
events.summary:
Netanyahu says ‘harsh disciplinary action’ will be taken after IDF confirms sledgehammer photo is authentic

  • Middle East crisis – live updates

The Israeli military is conducting a criminal investigation after a soldier was photographed striking a Catholic statue of Jesus with a sledgehammer in southern Lebanon.

Israel’s military officials said they had determined that an image circulating on social media showing the incident was authentic. The image appears to show an Israeli soldier using a sledgehammer to strike the head of a statue of a crucified Jesus that had fallen off a cross.

Continue reading...

events.title: Pakistan seeks to raise its global standing in push for Middle East peace
events.summary:
Islamabad has seized chance to act as mediator in Iran war and hopes to reap diplomatic and economic benefits

  • Middle East crisis – live updates

As Pakistan works frantically to narrow differences between Iran and the US in its newfound role as global peacemaker, it is also seeking to recast its diplomatic standing and attract business.

Pakistani officials, mediating between an unpredictable US president and hardliners in Tehran, were on Monday trying to coax both sides to put the conditions in place for a second round of talks in Islamabad this week, including easing the standoff in the strait of Hormuz. Pakistan was optimistic that the meeting would happen, viewing objections voiced by the Iranian side and Donald Trump’s threats as posturing for domestic audiences.

Continue reading...

events.title: US urges contractor to evacuate workers from Kuwait and Iraq over worries of Iran-backed attacks
events.summary:
After Guardian reports about danger to V2X employees, sources say state department raised concerns with defense contractor

  • Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox

The US government has called on the defense contractor V2X to evacuate its employees from Kuwait and Iraq, warning the company that they could be targeted by Iran-backed militias, four sources said.

The intervention follows reporting by the Guardian that V2X employees were stationed at US military bases in Kuwait, and at Martyr Brigadier General Ali Flaih airbase and Erbil in Iraq. Employees claimed having inadequate protections, receiving limited communications from the company about evacuation plans and being pressured to remain in the Middle East. In Iraq, workers say they are targets of Iran-allied attacks, and one employee was killed in a night-time drone attack in March.

Continue reading...

events.title: Myanmar military regime widens sanitary towel ban, claiming rebels use them for first aid
events.summary:
Activists say clamp down on period products to target insurgents is gender-based violence and violates rights

Myanmar’s military regime is expanding its ban on the distribution of period products, claiming they are being used to treat wounded resistance fighters, according to local activists.

The south-east Asian country has been locked in civil war since 2021, when the military usurped the democratic government and launched a violent crackdown on dissidents. Artillery fire, the burning of townships and arbitrary arrests have become common in the years since then.

Continue reading...

events.title: India fails to pass bill to boost women’s representation after delimitation row
events.summary:
Opposition accuses Narendra Modi government of using quotas as cover for redrawing electoral map

The Indian government has failed to pass a bill to increase female representation in parliament after being accused of using the plan as a guise to redraw the country’s electoral map.

It was the first time in 12 years in power that a constitutional amendment proposed by Narendra Modi’s Bharatiya Janata party (BJP) government was not passed by parliament.

Continue reading...

events.title: About 250 missing after boat carrying Rohingya refugees capsizes in Andaman Sea
events.summary:
Trawler set off from Bangladesh and reportedly capsized due to heavy winds, rough seas and overcrowding

About 250 people are missing after a boat carrying Rohingya refugees and Bangladeshi nationals capsized in the Andaman Sea, according to the UN’s refugee and migration agencies.

The agencies said the trawler carrying more than 250 men, women and children reportedly sank due to harsh weather and overcrowding. It had departed from Teknaf in southern Bangladesh and was bound for Malaysia.

Continue reading...

events.title: Indian music legend Asha Bhosle dies aged 92
events.summary:
Two-time Grammy nominee was one of Bollywood’s most versatile and celebrated voices

The Indian singer Asha Bhosle, whose voice defined Bollywood cinema and whose career spanned almost eight decades, has died in Mumbai at the age of 92.

Bhosle, who recorded more than 12,000 songs, became her country’s pre-eminent exponent of playback singing – recording tracks that were then lip-synced on film by actors. She also boldly embraced cabaret and western-influenced melodies to forge a distinctive musical identity.

Continue reading...

events.title: Islamabad prepares to host historic negotiations between Iran and the US
events.summary:
In Pakistan’s capital, the army has been deployed, a public holiday has been declared and the streets are eerily empty

  • Middle East crisis – live updates

The streets of Islamabad were on strict lockdown as Pakistan’s capital prepared to play host to historic negotiations between Iran and the US that have dangled the promise of an end to war that has devastated the Middle East.

Even as the US-Iran ceasefire looked increasingly precarious, amid Israel’s continued bombardment of Lebanon and disputes over the terms of the talks, Pakistani officials insisted that the make-or-break peace negotiations would be going ahead over the weekend as planned.

Continue reading...

events.title: Starmer accuses Robbins of obstructing truth about Mandelson vetting
events.summary:
PM admits he made mistake in choice of ambassador as he makes high-stakes statement to parliament over scandal

Keir Starmer has accused Olly Robbins of deliberately and repeatedly obstructing the truth about the Mandelson vetting scandal before a high-jeopardy appearance of the sacked top official before MPs on Tuesday.

Six days after the prime minister said he had learned that his pick for Washington ambassador had failed security vetting, Starmer admitted his decision to appoint him had been a fundamental mistake.

Continue reading...

events.title: Police investigate whether London arson attacks were planned for weeks
events.summary:
Criminals paid on behalf of Iran are believed to be behind attacks against Jewish targets, say detectives

Detectives are investigating whether the series of arson attacks in London was planned for weeks with suspects carrying out reconnaissance on the Jewish targets to be firebombed.

The series of attacks against synagogues and other Jewish targets, as well as one premises linked to Iranian dissidents, are believed to be carried out by criminals paid on behalf of Iran, police said.

Continue reading...

events.title: Mobile phones to be banned in schools in England under new plans
events.summary:
Government amendment to children’s wellbeing and schools bill to replace existing guidance with statutory ban

A ban on mobile phones in schools in England is to be introduced by the government to ensure that “critical safeguarding legislation” is passed.

The government will table an amendment to the children’s wellbeing and schools bill in the House of Lords after the bill was held up by peers on opposition benches.

Continue reading...

events.title: Baby was sexually abused before being killed by man adopting him, court told
events.summary:
Boy died aged 13 months after ‘routine abuse’ by Jamie Varley and his partner, John McGowan-Fazakerley, jury hears

A baby boy was “routinely ill-treated, sexually abused and physically assaulted” before he was killed by a secondary school teacher adopting him, a jury has heard.

Jamie Varley, the teacher, 37, and his partner, John McGowan-Fazakerley, 32, were in the process of adopting Preston Davey.

Continue reading...

events.title: Woman charged with attempted murder after car hits pedestrians in London
events.summary:
Gabrielle Carrington, 29, due to appear in court on Tuesday following incident in West End in early hours of Sunday

A woman has been charged with attempted murder after a car hit pedestrians in central London in the early hours of Sunday.

A woman in her 30s remains in a life-threatening condition and a man in his 50s suffered life-changing injuries after they were hit by a car in Argyll Street, Westminster, at approximately 4.30am on Sunday, the Metropolitan police said. A second woman in her 30s suffered minor injuries, the force added.

Continue reading...

events.title: Democrats say Trump administration ‘imploding’ after third cabinet member steps down – live
events.summary:
Lori Chavez-DeRemer departs administration just weeks after Kristi Noem and Pam Bondi also exited from their roles

  • Sign up for the Breaking News US email

My colleague Jeremy Barr has more on the lawsuit filed by Kash Patel against the Atlantic.

Patel’s legal team accused the magazine and reporter Sarah Fitzpatrick of publishing “a sweeping, malicious, and defamatory hit piece” on 17 April.

Continue reading...

events.title: Singer D4vd pleads not guilty to murder of 14-year-old girl found in his car
events.summary:
Musician charged after the dismembered and decomposing body of Celeste Rivas Hernandez found in abandoned Tesla

The singer D4vd pleaded not guilty to the murder of Celeste Rivas Hernandez, the teenage girl whose dismembered and decomposed body was found in the artist’s apparently abandoned Tesla in September.

The 21-year-old, whose legal name is David Burke, was arraigned on Monday afternoon hours after Los Angeles county district attorney’s office announced the charges against him.

Continue reading...

events.title: Trump labor secretary Lori Chavez-DeRemer resigns amid misconduct investigation
events.summary:
Chavez-DeRemer, entangled in string of controversies, leaving for private sector, president’s spokesperson says

Donald Trump’s labor secretary Lori Chavez-DeRemer is stepping down, the administration announced Monday.

“Labor Secretary Lori Chavez-DeRemer will be leaving the Administration to take a position in the private sector,” Trump spokesperson Steven Cheung wrote on social media. “She has done a phenomenal job in her role by protecting American workers, enacting fair labor practices, and helping Americans gain additional skills to improve their lives.”

Continue reading...

events.title: Tim Cook to step down as CEO of Apple: ‘the greatest privilege of my life’
events.summary:
Cook, who will assume the role of executive chair, will be succeeded by John Ternus as CEO on 1 September

Apple announced on Monday that it had named a replacement for Tim Cook as CEO, with head of hardware engineering John Ternus succeeding him on 1 September. Cook will stay at the company in the role of executive chair.

“It has been the greatest privilege of my life to be the CEO of Apple and to have been trusted to lead such an extraordinary company. I love Apple with all of my being,” Cook said in a press release.

Continue reading...

events.title: Federal agents detain wife of another US army member: ‘ICE is out of control’
events.summary:
Jose Serrano, a sergeant, said Deisy Rivera Ortega, his wife, was arrested at an immigration appointment

A US army sergeant with 27 years of military service – including deployment to Afghanistan – has said that federal immigration agents recently arrested his wife during an appointment at an immigration office in El Paso, Texas.

In an interview with CBS News published Monday, Sgt First Class Jose Serrano said that Deisy Rivera Ortega, a Salvadoran and his wife, was detained by Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) officers under the command of the Trump administration despite receiving legal protection in 2019 that bars her from being deported to El Salvador. Rivera Ortega, who wed Serrano in 2022, has been in the US since 2016, he said.

Continue reading...

events.title: U.S.-Iran talks are uncertain as ceasefire is set to expire
events.summary: As the clock ticks down on the ceasefire, Iran says it may not attend fresh talks with the U.S. That's as a temporary ceasefire between Israel and Hezbollah in Lebanon appears to be holding.

events.title: Peace talks are in doubt as the U.S. seizes an Iranian ship
events.summary: President Trump said a U.S. delegation will head to Pakistan to resume talks to end the war with Iran, but Tehran expressed reluctance after the U.S. seized one of its cargo ships in the Strait of Hormuz.

events.title: U.S. seizes Iranian cargo ship. And, tariff refund portal launches
events.summary: U.S. forces seized an Iranian-flagged cargo vessel in the Strait of Hormuz. And, an online government portal for processing tariff refunds launches today.

events.title: After massive cuts, U.S. says HIV work abroad is going well, but experts disagree
events.summary: The Trump administration has released new data on the country's HIV/AIDS work abroad. It is claiming the numbers are good news, but many HIV experts say they paint a worrisome picture.

events.title: Ukraine's fast-growing defense tech sector takes center stage in Iran war
events.summary: While Ukraine seeks to shift its image from not only a recipient of military aid but also a provider, its battle-tested, anti-drone tech is taking center stage in the Iran war.

events.title: A humanoid robot sprints past the human half-marathon world record in Beijing race
events.summary: The winner from Honor, a Chinese smartphone maker, completed the race in 50 minutes and 26 seconds, running faster than the human world record in a show of China's technological leaps.

events.title: U.K police investigating if attacks in London are the work of Iranian proxies
events.summary: U.K. police said Sunday they are investigating whether a string of arson attacks on Jewish sites in London are the work of Iranian proxies, the latest of which caused minor damage on Saturday night.

events.title: U.S. says it seized an Iranian cargo ship, ceasefire expires soon
events.summary: The two-week ceasefire between the US and Iran is set to expire Wednesday. And the two sides seem far from a resolution. President Trump says the US has taken custody of an Iranian vessel.

events.title: U.S. seizes Iranian cargo ship in Strait of Hormuz
events.summary: The U.S. has taken custody of an Iranian-flagged cargo ship in the Strait of Hormuz after firing on its engine room, President Trump said in a post on social media.

events.title: North Korea launches ballistic missiles toward sea
events.summary: South Korea's Joint Chiefs of Staff says the launches happened on Sunday morning from the North's eastern Sinpo area.

events.title: Tim Cook to step down as Apple CEO, John Ternus to take over
events.summary: John Ternus, who has long focused ​on design and ‌products at Apple, will lead the iPhone maker as it gears up for an industry change spurred by artificial intelligence.

events.title: Mexico: Canadian killed in shouting at Teotihuacan pyramid
events.summary: A gunman opened fire at the popular tourist and archeological site outside of Mexico city.

events.title: UK: Starmer says he would't have appointed Mandelson as US envoy if he'd known he failed vetting process
events.summary: Under-fire Prime Minister Keir Starmer told the House of Commons that neither he nor his ministers were told that the former ambassador to the US and Epstein associate Peter Mandelson had failed his security vetting.

events.title: Elon Musk skips probe as France investigates X
events.summary: French prosecutors are investigating whether content generated by X's AI chatbot Grok contributed to the spread of child sexual abuse material, explicit deepfakes and Holocaust denial.

events.title: Ugandan military reports rescue of some 200 hostages from ADF militants in eastern DRC
events.summary: Uganda's military has said that it and forces from the neighboring Democratic Republic of Congo freed some 200 hostages in eastern DRC in a joint operation against Islamist militants known as the ADF.

events.title: Unpacking the 2026 Venice Biennale controversies and highlights
events.summary: Russia's return to the international contemporary art exhibition, as well as the participation of Israel and the US, have sparked politicized backlash.

events.title: Sudan war enters fourth year as child hunger crisis deepens
events.summary: Sudan’s war has displaced millions, leaving parts of the country facing famine. Aid agencies warn children are bearing the brunt as food shortages worsen and humanitarian funding declines.

events.title: Is To Lam's Vietnam turning into Xi Jinping's China?
events.summary: After Vietnam's lawmakers unanimously elected Communist Party chief To Lam as the country's president, analysts are asking whether Vietnam aims to copy China's political model.

events.title: Bundesliga debut by female coach marred by misogyny
events.summary: Marie-Louise Eta's historic debut as a head coach in the men's Bundesliga ended in defeat. While her appointment is a sign of progress, the reactions to it leave no doubt about how much is still to be done.

events.title: South Korea, India agree to boost trade ties amid global tensions
events.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi hosts South Korean President Lee Jae Myung this week as both countries target $50 billion in trade by 2030.

events.title: Bulgaria enters uncharted territory as Radev wins big
events.summary: Preliminary results show that former President Rumen Radev has won Bulgaria's general election with a clear majority and the biggest result for a single party since 1997. Will Bulgaria finally get a stable government?

events.title: How China's chip expansion puts pressure on global rivals
events.summary: US curbs on advanced chips pushed China to focus on its own semiconductor ecosystem. And while it trails at the very cutting edge, China's "good‑enough" technology is fast powering much of the global economy.

events.title: Japan issues tsunami alert after strong earthquake
events.summary: The 7.4-magnitude quake off northern Iwate prefecture could be felt as far as Tokyo. Residents have been urged to evacuate northern coastal regions ahead of a tsunami.

events.title: Bulgaria: Pro-Russia Rumen Radev wins ‌election
events.summary: Rumen Radev's new center-left party, Progressive Bulgaria has won an absolute majority in the country's eighth general election in five years. Both Russia and the EU have already sent their congratulations.

events.title: Ukraine hits Russian Black Sea port city, 1 dead
events.summary: Russian authorities said one man was killed in a Ukrainian drone raid on city of Tuapse. Meanwhile, Russia arrested a German woman in an alleged bomb plot.

events.title: Iran war: 'No plans' for fresh peace talks, Tehran says
events.summary: Pakistan is preparing to host a second round of negotiations, but doubts remain as to whether they will take place, after the US seizure of an Iranian cargo ship. DW has the latest.

events.title: US strike on alleged drug boat in Caribbean kills 3
events.summary: Trump has said that the US is in "armed conflict" with cartels, as its military campaign has killed at least 180 people since last year amid criticism over its legality.

events.title: Germany news: Timmy the stranded whale back on the move
events.summary: The struggling humpback whale is due to be fitted with a radio transmitter to help follow his recovery. Meanwhile, Chancellor Merz has promised that Germany is prepared for any crisis. DW has more.

events.title: Will Venezuela's oil sector reform attract investors?
events.summary: After the US ousted Maduro and effectively seized control of Venezuela's oil industry, the country's economy has been transitioning rapidly. The interim government is wooing investors but lacks democratic backing.

events.title: The German refugees who found shelter in Yugoslavia
events.summary: After the Nazis seized power in 1933, many Germans escaped by fleeing abroad. Some ended up in what was then Yugoslavia. German historian Marie Janine Calic tells their story.

events.title: Bayern Munich beat Stuttgart to clinch Bundesliga title
events.summary: Bayern Munich are German league champions for a record-extending 35th time after beating Stuttgart 4-2. They have now won 13 of the past 14 Bundesliga titles.

events.title: Life in Donbas: 'If we give up, there will be nothing left'
events.summary: The Russian army is advancing on the Ukrainian town of Kostiantynivka, with the aim of occupying the entire Donbas region. Yet people continue to live and fight in the area, as DW's Hanna Sokolova-Stekh found out.

events.title: Germany News: Neighbor attacked for pounding schnitzel too loudly
events.summary: Police in southern Germany were called in to settle a schnitzel dispute that had turned violent, with a man reportedly slapping his neighbor for pounding his schnitzel too loudly.

events.title: Climate change impacts India's harvest festivals
events.summary: As people across India celebrate traditional agrarian spring festivals, climate change has become an unwanted guest at the table. How are communities rising to the pressure on harvests, water, and rural life?

events.title: French billionaire defiant as authors quit top publisher
events.summary: Vincent Bollore has vowed to find new authors for publisher Grasset after 170 left, alleging political interference. French President Macron has weighed in on the affair, which has shaken France's cultural scene.

events.title: German crime figures: Are migrants unfairly targeted?
events.summary: Is it true that the crime rate in Germany is higher among immigrants? Statistics suggest as much, but numbers by themselves can be misleading. An expert explains what's behind them.

events.title: Austria: Police find rat poison in recalled baby food jar
events.summary: The Germany-based HiPP had recalled the jars, suggesting they were tampered with. The contaminated jars were also found in the Czech Republic and Slovakia.

events.title: Hannover Messe 2026: Brazilian flair awaits visitors
events.summary: With war in Iran on hold, the world's largest industrial trade fair opens in Hannover. And Brazil, this year's partner country, is pulling out all stops to convince visitors it's a reliable technology leader.

events.title: The German who recovered from a stroke to seal Olympic first
events.summary: Kathrin Marchand was an elite rower for years before becoming a doctor. But a stroke at the age of 30 changed everything. Despite the challenges, she has now achieved something no other Olympian ever has.

events.title: Record fuel prices: How are governments responding?
events.summary: In Germany, a liter of diesel costs up to 40% more than before the Iran war and other countries have seen even bigger price jumps. What are governments around the world doing to combat record fuel prices?

events.title: Europe looks to secure shipping in Strait of Hormuz
events.summary: A coalition of nonbelligerent states led by France and the UK has said it is ready to lead an international mission to restore navigation in the Strait of Hormuz. But that won't be deployed before the Iran war is over.

events.title: Rutte dismisses speculation about US leaving NATO
events.summary: NATO chief Mark Rutte said he understands Donald Trump's frustration with NATO. He also called on Europe to boost its defense industry.

events.title: Teen discovers first ancient Greek artifact found in Berlin
events.summary: A 13-year-old stumbled upon a coin from ancient Troy — an extremely rare find in Germany. Experts say it may offer new clues about little-known links between ancient Greece and northern Europe.

events.title: Italy's Meloni breaks with Trump over war in Iran, pope
events.summary: The war in Iran and a spat between two of the world's most influential Americans has forced Giorgia Meloni to choose sides, potentially pushing the Italian prime minister to align herself with Europe's center-right.

events.title: Cyprus: Global politics felt at Candidates chess tournaments
events.summary: The FIDE Candidates tournaments are two of the most important events in the chess calendar. This year, they took place in Cyprus. But tension relating to Russia and the war in the Middle East was never too far away.

events.title: Chess: Uzbekistan's new star shows Asia's continued rise
events.summary: Javokhir Sindarov of Uzbekistan has earned the right to challenge India's Dommaraju Gukesh for the world title. Both men are under 21 and from Asia, underlining an ongoing demographic shift in top level chess.

events.title: Nueva Germania: The failed 'Aryan project' in Paraguay
events.summary: A new home for the "Aryan race." That was what German emigrants envisioned when they founded Nueva Germania in Paraguay in 1886. It failed back then but a version of the town still exists today.

events.title: Will Bulgaria's election change the country's course?
events.summary: Bulgarians will vote in their eighth parliamentary election in five years on April 19. Former President Rumen Radev is projected to win. Radev, who has a record of pro-Russian stances, is pledging to fight corruption.

events.title: Several killed in Russian strikes across Ukraine, many more injured
events.summary: A 12-year-old was among those killed as Russian missile and drone attacks hit Ukrainian cities of Kyiv, Odesa and Dnipro.

events.title: Senegal: The Goethe-Institut opens its new building in Dakar
events.summary: The Goethe-Institut's new building in Dakar champions sustainable architecture, made from the earth. Star architect Francis Kere has blended local materials with contemporary design.

events.title: Anatomy of the clitoris: 3D images reveal its nerve system
events.summary: New 3D images reveal the clitoris’s complex nerve network, highlighting long‑standing gaps in medical understanding of the female body.

events.title: Top EU court rules online gamblers can sue for compensation if betting illegal in home country
events.summary: The ECJ has ruled that online gamblers can seek compensation if they lost money when gambling was illegal in their home country, even if the laws have since changed. This could pave the way for others to reclaim losses.

events.title: Germany-Israel divide deepens after Merz criticism
events.summary: The Israeli finance minister's sharp criticism of the German chancellor's views on Israel's settlement policy is just the latest sign of a growing estrangement. What's the current state of German-Israeli relations?

events.title: US jury finds Live Nation, Ticketmaster hold illegal monopoly
events.summary: The verdict will not bring immediate relief to concertgoers frustrated by high ticket prices, but could cost the company hundreds of millions of dollars.

events.title: World Cup 2026: Fans with disabilities 'excluded'
events.summary: Complaints that the 2026 World Cup, hosted largely by the USA, is a rip off have been widespread and constant. But it's not just the prices that are making it feel impossible for football fans with disabilities.

events.title: Germany news: Teacher group urges action over pupil violence
events.summary: A German teachers' group says decisive action is needed to counter a surge in violence in classrooms and corridors. Meanwhile, the Greens say a blanket speed limit on major highways would help save fuel.

events.title: UK signs deal to rejoin EU's Erasmus exchange scheme
events.summary: Britain will rejoin the popular Europe-wide student exchange program six years after leaving it.

events.title: Germany, the UK warn the Iran war distracts from Ukraine and oil price rises help Russia
events.summary: Germany's defense minister warned that Russia "benefits from current developments in the Middle East," as the Ukraine Defense Contact Group met in Berlin. He said rising oil prices were filling Moscow's "war coffers."

events.title: Berlin hosts Sudan conference amid brutal, forgotten war
events.summary: The war in Sudan has been raging for the past three years, and a ceasefire is nowhere in sight. Participants at a conference of donors in Berlin hope at least to ease the suffering of the people caught in the middle.

events.title: German health minister announces billions in cutbacks
events.summary: The comprehensive reform package aims to plug a multibillion-euro hole in Germany's expensive health care system. Reforms include mandatory second opinions for costly surgeries and no more coverage for homeopathy.

events.title: Solar power in Morocco's desert: Bold vision, mixed results
events.summary: A massive solar tower in the Moroccan desert is the beacon of an ambitious push for a clean energy future. But fossil fuels and grid constraints stand in the way.

events.title: Women's workwear: When proper fit becomes a safety issue
events.summary: With limited options, many women in trades and industry have to wear men's workwear. But a poor fit causes discomfort and can compromise safety. Now, some firms are stepping up to design safer, better-fitting gear.

events.title: What we call sex work — and what it says about society
events.summary: A Bonn exhibition traces the cultural history of sex work and shows how the words used to describe it have shaped stigma, policy and lived experience.

events.title: Germany: Man found guilty in Pelicot-style rape case
events.summary: A Chinese student has been found guilty of drugging and raping a woman in Munich over a period of months. The perpetrator was a member of a Telegram chat group where men organized rapes and shared images of their crimes.

events.title: Hungary: What's next for Peter Magyar after election win?
events.summary: Peter Magyar has promised nothing less than a "system change" in Hungary. The expectations both at home and abroad are huge. DW explores what Magyar is likely to do on the most pressing issues facing Hungary.

events.title: France's Renault slashes engineer jobs amid pressure from Chinese carmakers
events.summary: The French carmaker said it would cut up to 2,400 engineering jobs as the company tries to catch up to Chinese electric vehicles in terms of price and production speed.

events.title: Spain: Sanchez government finalizes amnesty plan for undocumented migrants
events.summary: Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez's government hailed the plan as "an act of justice and a necessity." It will allow unauthorized migrants to apply for temporary residence permits instead, and to start paying taxes.

events.title: Lufthansa pilots to strike again for 2 more days, leading to a full working week of disruption
events.summary: Pilots at Germany's Lufthansa airline will strike again on Thursday and Friday, the Vereinigung Cockpit trade union said. Cabin crew are also striking this week, clouding the company's centenary celebrations

events.title: Sudan: Iran war prompts 'massive' food, fuel price rises; drones kill hundreds of civilians in 2026
events.summary: German charities warn of increases of 70-80% in the costs of essentials in wartorn Sudan amid the unrest in the Middle East. Donors gather in Berlin on Wednesday on the third anniversary of the outbreak of the war.

events.title: Germany's aid to Ukraine faces challenges
events.summary: At Ukrainian-German government talks, Berlin reassures Kyiv it can continue counting on military, civilian, and humanitarian aid, even as political opposition and US cuts create challenges for sustained support.

events.title: Germany debates rape law to tackle AI and online abuse
events.summary: Calls are growing for Germany to update its rape law after a celebrity case involving "deepfakes" caused an uproar. Campaigners are also pushing for a move to the "only yes means yes" principle of active consent.

events.title: Hungary: Orban election loss reverberates across Central Europe
events.summary: Viktor Orban's crushing defeat in the April 12 election will have far-reaching consequences for Europe's political landscape — and especially for neighboring Czechia and Slovakia, which are run by his political allies.

events.title: The world ditched wasteful toilets, the US stayed behind
events.summary: US toilets use far more water than many of their global counterparts. President Donald Trump is pushing to loosen water pressure standards, a move critics say would increase waste.

events.title: Spanish PM Sanchez' wife charged with corruption
events.summary: The case is based on a complaint by an anti-corruption group with far-right ties, and is the latest in a string of corruption allegations which have plagued Sanchez' inner circle.

events.title: EU applauds Hungary's election result
events.summary: After Hungary’s opposition ousted Viktor Orban after 16 years in power, hopes rise in Brussels that Budapest may abandon its obstructionist course. What is the EU expecting from Peter Magyar?

events.title: Lufthansa pilots launch 2-day strike, cabin crew say they will strike right after
events.summary: Pilots who are members of the Vereinigung Cockpit trade union are striking on Monday and Tuesday. As hundreds of flights were canceled, the UFO cabin crew union said it would also strike on Wednesday and Thursday.

events.title: Trump's Strait of Hormuz blockade hits Iran's oil trade
events.summary: A US naval blockade cuts off Iran's main source of hard currency by halting most of its oil exports. Will President Trump's pressure force Tehran back to negotiations or widen the conflict?

events.title: Hungary: Orban's election defeat sparks hope and relief
events.summary: Viktor Orban's Fidesz party was swept aside in Hungary's parliamentary election. His defeat is a blow for Putin, Trump and populists the world over. Election winner Peter Magyar is promising major changes.

events.title: Germany's Buchenwald: Remembering Nazi atrocities
events.summary: Buchenwald was one of the largest concentration camps on German soil. Eighty‑one years ago, it was liberated. The commemoration included moving words — and protests.

events.title: World's oldest gorilla celebrates 69th birthday at Berlin Zoo
events.summary: Lady Fatou, known as the "grand dame" of the Berlin Zoo, was certified last year by Guinness as the oldest living gorilla in the world.

events.title: Germany news: Merz says fuel tax lowered to ease price surge
events.summary: Berlin is cutting fuel tax and arms manufacturer Rheinmetall is preparing to build missiles in a new venture. Elsewhere, a fisherman triggered a massive search before being found drunk and asleep in a car. DW has more.

events.title: Hungary: Peter Magyar's election heralds thaw in EU ties
events.summary: Following the ouster of Russia-friendly Viktor Orban after 16 years, Europe is now waiting to see if his designated successor Peter Magyar can make good on his promises to repair ties with Brussels.

events.title: Merck's Keytruda: A lifesaving drug, a global divide
events.summary: A cross-border investigation by DW and ICIJ reveals how pricing and patents helped turn a life-saving medicine into one of the world's best selling drugs while leaving many patients struggling to access it.

events.title: Boosting women's role in politics, Dutch-style
events.summary: Some 504 women were elected in recent Dutch local elections, with a "Vote for a Woman" campaign boosting their chances to gather preferential votes. Despite the record figure, women remain underrepresented.

events.title: Germany: Betting on elections is illegal — but possible
events.summary: Online gambling is gaining traction in Germany. Using prediction markets like Polymarket to bet on political events remains illegal, but it is almost impossible to police.

events.title: UFOs: 'Most are reported by people out for an evening smoke'
events.summary: Hansjürgen Köhler has been a volunteer UFO hunter for more than 50 years. He sees his work as scientific investigation, but sometimes also as caring for others.

events.title: Is this Syrian doctor one of the 80% Germany wants to remove?
events.summary: Chancellor Friedrich Merz has ignited a debate by declaring that 80% of Syrians would be repatriated. Does this mean that Germany also intends to deport those who are integrated and whom the country needs?

events.title: First gay rights movement: Berlin's wild 1920s queer history
events.summary: Before the Nazis took power, Berlin was a center of LGBTQ+ rights and queer culture, with research institutes, a flourishing nightlife and one of the earliest gay rights' movements.

events.title: NASA's billion-dollar space race goes into overdrive
events.summary: NASA's renewed push to get a human mission to the moon and Mars is neither cheap, nor for the faint of heart. But who ends up covering the costs and what does everyone still on Earth get out of all this?

events.title: Recycling's next big thing — or big bluff?
events.summary: An advanced recycling process claims it can handle hard-to-recycle plastics, like packaging. But critics say it's not worth the effort.

events.title: Has Coachella festival sold out?
events.summary: One of the world's biggest open-air music and art festivals has been hit by controversies and declining credibility — yet the world's biggest acts continue to headline.

events.title: Veteran German actor Mario Adorf dies aged 95
events.summary: Few actors had a career as rich as Mario Adorf. He played legendary Hollywood villains, wrote German TV history and still had film roles at age 90.

events.title: Artemis II crew emotional as mission prepares for reentry
events.summary: In less than 48 hours, the Orion spacecraft is due to splash down in the Pacific Ocean. Crew members have said what they saw in space will forever change how they see the world.

events.title: Hungary election: How football has helped Orban keep power
events.summary: Viktor Orban is a football fanatic but has long used the sport as a political tool, both in Hungary and abroad. With polls predicting election defeat, he could also lose his chance of hosting the Champions League final.

events.title: Norway's Telenor faces lawsuit for giving junta data from its customers in Myanmar
events.summary: A Swedish non-profit has filed a class action lawsuit against Norway's Telenor, accusing it of endangering customers in Myanmar by sharing their data with the junta. The company said it had no real choice.

events.title: How farmers are rising to a fertilizer shortfall
events.summary: The war in Iran has sparked a global fertilizer shortage and is threatening food security. What are farmers around the world doing to save their crops?

events.title: Should Western influencers promote Taliban‑run Afghanistan?
events.summary: In the race for clicks, likes and followers, some travel content creators are being criticized for promoting Afghanistan while ignoring the harsh reality of Taliban rule.

events.title: 60 years after the Indonesian mass killings: Is the Cold War back?
events.summary: Six decades after helping the Indonesian army crush one of the biggest communist parties in the world, the US is once again meddling in countries it considers a thorn in its flesh. Is history repeating itself?

events.title: Will AI doom office space to history?
events.summary: Artificial intelligence's potential for disruption is being felt across multiple industries. Now commercial real estate is feeling the heat from what analysts say is a "paradigm shift."

events.title: Homegrown energy is slashing US energy bills
events.summary: In times of skyrocketing energy prices, some US citizens are making big savings by producing electricity at home.

events.title: One in five young Germans plan to leave the country
events.summary: A recent study shows that as many as one in five Germans between the ages of 14 and 29 are actively thinking about moving to another country — and 40% plan to do so in the long term.

events.title: Can small nuclear reactors solve Europe's energy woes?
events.summary: Supporters say modular reactors are key to meeting energy goals, but critics say they're a costly distraction.

events.title: Mediterranean sharks are vanishing in a legal void
events.summary: Along Libya's coast, sharks — including breeding females — are being sold unchecked. With little enforcement and growing financial pressure on fishermen, vulnerable species are spiraling toward extinction.

events.title: 'Drill baby drill' ― Trump opens up nature to big energy
events.summary: America's beloved national parks and public lands face conservation rollbacks and sell-offs as the administration of US President Donald Trump pushes for fossil fuel and timber extraction.

events.title: Semenya hits out at impact of Olympic ban on DSD athletes
events.summary: Caster Semenya has had her gender scrutinized in public for years after winning two Olympic golds. She has hit out at what she sees as a policy that disproportionately affects athletes with sexual differences.

events.title: Wealth tax: Why are countries afraid to tax the ultrarich?
events.summary: Growing income inequality and public displays of billionaire power are increasing calls for new income and wealth taxes aimed at the world's richest individuals. Can wealth ever be taxed fairly?

events.title: How US 'Liberation Day' tariffs reshaped global trade
events.summary: One year after Trump's "Liberation Day" tariffs, global trade flows have shifted. Trade data shows which countries gained, which lost — and who is footing the bill.

events.title: UNESCO World Heritage sites facing the heat
events.summary: From Easter Island to the Great Wall of China, many of the world's most iconic cultural heritage sites are under threat from rising temperatures linked to climate change.

events.title: World Cup 2026: Iraq qualify but Iran doubts loom
events.summary: Iraq have overcome tough circumstances to reach the World Cup for the first time in 30 years, with the lineup now complete. But Iran's potential withdrawal still hangs over a tournament fraught with political issues.

events.title: FIFA World Cup 2026: Trump's politics put off German fans
events.summary: Many German fans are torn on whether to travel to the US for this summer's major football tournament.

events.title: Tiger Woods 'stepping away' for treatment after DUI arrest
events.summary: Tiger Woods has announced a break from golf four days after he crashed his SUV and was arrested for driving under influence. Fans had been awaiting his return at the Masters this month.

events.title: Were Grandma and Grandpa Nazis?
events.summary: What did our forebears do during the Nazi era? A subject long shrouded in silence in German families. Now, the US National Archives has made millions of NSDAP membership cards available online.

events.title: FIFA World Cup: Which German players will make the final cut?
events.summary: Several players on Germany's national football team have really stood out during the squad's recent test matches ahead of this summer's major soccer tournament.

events.title: After the Iran war, how fast could global trade recover?
events.summary: The Iran war could end soon as a ceasefire goes into effect. But the true test will be how quickly energy, fertilizer and other supply chains can bounce back whenever the Strait of Hormuz is reopened.

events.title: Germany beat Ghana to complete confidence-boosting March
events.summary: A late winner from Stuttgart's Deniz Undav secured a second friendly win in a week for Germany. With the FIFA World Cup just 10 weeks away, Julian Nagelsmann's team will be starting to believe they can win it all.

events.title: Air Canada CEO to retire after English-only condolence furor
events.summary: Air Canada has announced that CEO Michael Rousseau will retire later this year. This follows public criticism of his failure to voice condolences in French as well as English after two pilots were killed in a collision.

events.title: The pleasure of books in the digital age
events.summary: The digitization of global literature has brought vast libraries to our fingertips. But the quiet power of a physical book offers a rare escape from constant digital noise.

events.title: Amnesty flags FIFA World Cup human rights risks
events.summary: The human rights group has sounded the alarm about risks faced by visitors to the 2026 FIFA World Cup. It goes as far as to describe the situation in the United States as a "human rights emergency."

events.title: Solar is winning the energy race
events.summary: The world's cheapest power source is scaling at warp speed, pushing coal, gas and nuclear aside.

events.title: At 40, Lady Gaga isn't done revolutionizing pop music
events.summary: As iconic as she is outlandish, Lady Gaga has left quite a mark on the world of music. Here's how she reinvented pop — and reached millions by opening up about her trauma and illness.

events.title: Florian Wirtz inspires Germany past Switzerland
events.summary: Germany beat Switzerland 4-3 in an entertaining World Cup warm-up in Basel. Two goals and two assists from Liverpool's Florian Wirtz secured a confidence-boosting victory for Julian Nagelsmann's team.

events.title: Daylight saving time: A history of fact, folklore and fuss
events.summary: From wartime strategy to Halloween lobbying lore, the legacy of daylight saving time is tangled in culture, commerce and contested claims.

events.title: Fighting in Iran: Is another 1970s‑style oil crisis looming?
events.summary: Supply disruptions, high prices, nervous markets: The current war in the Middle East brings back memories of the oil crises of the 1970s. Back then, many economies plunged into stagflation. How bad is the current crisis?

events.title: 'Looksmaxxing' — the manosphere beauty cult
events.summary: The online trend with roots in misogyny encourages socially isolated young men to undertake painful practices to gain beauty and status — and its language is reaching the White House.

events.title: Smart sleepers could be the glow-up night trains need
events.summary: Europe has a bit of an on-off relationship with night trains. A Berlin team's smart cabin concept could lead to greater commitment.

events.title: World Cup 2026: Cost, Trump and travel give fans pause
events.summary: As teams battle it out for the final six spots, loyal fans are making plans for World Cup 2026. DW spoke to supporters across the world to hear about five-figure expenditure, visa uncertainty and safety concerns.

events.title: Iran war boosts strategic significance of South Caucasus
events.summary: With war raging in Iran, the South Caucasus has become one of the few stable corridors between Asia and Europe. Nowhere are the conflict's opportunities and risks more visible than in Azerbaijan.

events.title: Germany's green reputation hits a crossroads
events.summary: Germany's new climate spend is big on wind power and e‑mobility. Some call it real momentum, others say it’s basically a green band‑aid.

events.title: Is VW eyeing Iron Dome parts production at German plant?
events.summary: A report in the Financial Times says Volkswagen is considering producing defense components for Israel. A VW spokesperson told DW the company was exploring "viable options."

events.title: Is Iran cashing in millions from Strait of Hormuz blockade?
events.summary: An Iranian lawmaker claims Tehran is quietly charging tankers up to $2 million for safe passage through the Strait of Hormuz. The move threatens to turn the key waterway into a high-stakes toll booth.

events.title: From Goethe to Soraya: German-Iranian stories
events.summary: It's hard to imagine now, but Iran and Germany were once surprisingly close. A master poet, a German "Lawrence of Arabia," and an empress all played a role in this shared history.

events.title: WNBA's labor deal is a structural shift for women's sports
events.summary: America's top women's basketball league just agreed to a new CBA. It will have a significant impact on women's sport.

events.title: NASA plans moon base instead of orbital lunar station
events.summary: New NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman says the US space agency will pivot from launching an orbiting lunar station to constructing a base on the moon's surface. It comes amid a renewed moon race with Russia and China.

events.title: Who wins what in the EU's new trade deal with Australia?
events.summary: Squeezed by Trump's tariffs and China's industrial muscle, the EU and Australia bury old trade disputes to finalize a long-delayed free trade deal.

events.title: American apocalypse: The end 'feels personal and imminent'
events.summary: Social psychologists in the US found that a third of people surveyed feel the world will end in their lifetimes. That's not only depressing; it also affects people's sense of agency when faced by war or climate change.

events.title: Australia and EU strike free trade deal
events.summary: The European Union and Australia have agreed on a sweeping new trade deal, compromising to boost exports in the face of global uncertainty over trade.

events.title: Argentina's 'Dirty War' still on trial 50 years later
events.summary: Some 30,000 people were "disappeared" under the brutal military dictatorship that ruled Argentina from 1976 to 1983. Victims are still fighting for justice.

events.title: Report: Hungary using more Russian oil, despite EU phase out
events.summary: A new report by the Center for the Study of Democracy, shared exclusively with DW, says that Hungary has increased its dependence on Russian oil since 2021. It has also become highly dependent on Russian gas.

events.title: Asian markets tumble, but Europe, US rally on Trump optimism
events.summary: Markets in Asia nosedived as trading restarted on Monday. Europe's indices were headed in the same direction, until optimistic Iran comments from Donald Trump prompted traders in Europe and the US to start buying again.

events.title: Iranians and Israelis united through music
events.summary: The Berlin-based music ensemble Sistanagila brings together musicians from Iran and Israel who offer a message of hope for a better future.

events.title: Alexandra Popp and Borussia Dortmund: A sign of the times
events.summary: Germany's most famous female footballer has signed for a third-division team. The transfer will have huge impacts across the women's game in Germany.

events.title: Earth's climate more unbalanced than ever, WMO warns
events.summary: The world's oceans have broken heat records for nine straight years, glaciers are retreating and extreme weather is killing thousands. The only way to avoid the worst is to urgently ditch fossil fuels.

events.title: Navigation satellites guide the world — and its wars
events.summary: From smartphone maps to precision weapons, satellite navigation underpins modern life and war. Global navigation satellite systems have become indispensable, and that's made them vulnerable to disruption and attack.

events.title: Canadian tourist killed in Mexico archaeological site shooting
events.summary: A Canadian woman was shot dead and five other people injured by a gunman on Monday at Mexico's famed Teotihuacan archaeological site, authorities said. The gunman killed himself after opening fire at the popular tourist destination, home to pre-Aztecan pyramids, said Cristobal Castaneda, the security secretary for Mexico state, where the site is located. FRANCE 24's Ioan Grillo reports from Mexico City.

events.title: Cuba confirms meeting with US officials on island, wants energy blockade lifted
events.summary: Cuba’s government on Monday confirmed that it had recently met with U.S. officials on the island as tensions between the two sides remain high over the U.S. energy blockade of the Caribbean country. FRANCE 24's Ed Augustin reports from Havana.

events.title: Tim Cook to step down as Apple CEO in September after 15-year tenure
events.summary: Apple CEO Tim Cook will step down on Sept. 1 after nearly 15 years leading the iPhone maker, handing the role to hardware chief John Ternus. Cook, 65, will become executive chairman. Under his tenure, Apple’s market value rose by more than $3.6 trillion, marking a period of sustained growth.

events.title: Elon Musk snubs Paris prosecutors' summons over X and Grok
events.summary: Elon Musk did not appear Monday for a voluntary interview with Paris prosecutors, who had issued the summons in February as part of an investigation into allegations that X's algorithm was used to interfere in French politics. The probe was later expanded to include dissemination of Holocaust denial and sexual deepfakes by X's AI chatbot Grok. FRANCE 24's Sharon Gaffney speaks with Professor Julia Hörnle, Chair of Internet Law at Queen Mary, University of London.

events.title: Mythos shock - is AI taking cybersecurity risks to new levels?
events.summary: Governments and regulators are assessing potential implications and urging critical sectors to beef up their defences after a powerful new AI model sent shockwaves around the world. Mythos showed the ability to find previously undetected bugs and to exploit the vulnerabilities by itself, prompting its developer Anthropic to decide not to release it to the general public. Aleksandr Yampolskiy, Co-founder and CEO of SecurityScorecard, explains the inflection point the new AI model brings.

events.title: Starmer’s Mandelson 'gamble': Did political firepower trump diplomacy to appease Washington?
events.summary: François Picard is pleased to welcome Tim Bale, Professor of Politics at Queen Mary, University of London. According to Professor Bale, Britain's Prime Minister Keir Starmer appears to have unwittingly misled Parliament, as a consequence of not being not being 'apprised' of Peter Mandelson's failed vetting.

events.title: Shooter at Mexico's Teotihuacan pyramid site kills Canadian tourist, wounds four others
events.summary: A Canadian woman was killed and four other people were injured Monday at the archaeological site of the Teotihuacan pyramids, a major tourist attraction north of Mexico City. The gunman killed himself after opening fire, Mexico state's security secretary said.

events.title: EU to gauge member states' willingness to take measures against Israel
events.summary: EU member states will discuss their cooperation agreement with Israel, EU foreign policy chief Kaja Kallas said Monday, though she remained cautious about the outcome of the talks. Spain is leading the call to suspend the deal as attitudes towards Israel among key EU member states are hardening over its conduct in the Gaza Strip, Lebanon and the occupied West Bank.

events.title: Israel confirms its soldier destroyed Jesus statue in Lebanon
events.summary: The Israeli military confirmed a viral photo of one of its soldiers smashing a statue of Jesus with a sledgehammer in southern Lebanon is "authentic," and said its launched an investigation into the incident. Taken in the Christian village of Debel, close to the Israeli border, it's one of the few villages where residents remained during Israel's campaign in southern Lebanon. Locals say the statue was on a crucifix outside a family home.

events.title: 'Trump has boxed himself into a corner', expert says as fate of Iran peace talks uncertain
events.summary: Speaking with FRANCE 24's Jennie Shin, Rosemary Kelanic, Director of the Middle East programme at the think tank Defense Priorities, says that US President Donald Trump "has boxed himself into a corner", explaining that "this war has failed, the war has put the United States in a weaker position relative to Iran than it was before the war started, because now Iran has closed the Strait and demonstrated its ability to do that".

events.title: Iran says 'no decision' on joining new talks, with US delegation set to depart
events.summary: Iran said it had yet to decide whether to attend a new round of peace negotiations with the United States, as Washington's delegation prepared to depart for Pakistan on Monday. The uncertainty over the talks on ending the war that engulfed the region came as US President Donald Trump suggested he could resume attacks should the current temporary ceasefire -- now in its final days -- lapse. FRANCE 24's Reza Sayah reports from Tehran.

events.title: What exit strategy? Trump, Iranians send mixed signals as truce deadline looms
events.summary: A container ship seized in the Gulf, a ceasefire deadline that's running out fast and plenty of bargaining table brinkmanship between the U-S and Iran. With the price of oil back on the rise, de-escalation would certainly offer relief to Donald Trump's constitutents. So what's the plan of a U.S. president who’s proved impulsive – at times threatening civilizational erasure, at others floating the idea of splitting the profits with Iran on a Strait of Hormuz toll for tankers? How to bargain when your opponent enjoys home field advantage and knows you don’t want to put boots on the ground?

events.title: REPLAY: UK PM Starmer faces parliament over Mandelson vetting controversy
events.summary: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer acknowledged Monday that he made the wrong judgment when he appointed Peter Mandelson as UK ambassador to Washington, but said he would not have done so if he had known Mandelson failed security checks. Starmer is facing calls to resign after the revelation that Mandelson was given the job despite failing security vetting. Mandelson was fired nine months into the job over his friendship with Jeffrey Epstein.

events.title: 'Hierarchy of sovereignty': Israel claims authority over 'which Lebanese areas are inhabitable'
events.summary: Carys Garland is pleased to welcome Nadim Houry, Executive Director of the Arab Reform Initiative (ARI). According to Houry, the “Yellow Line doctrine” is not a security measure, but rather a military tool for territorial control. Drawing on recent developments in Gaza, Syria, and Lebanon, he argues that this doctrine operates as a legal façade for expanding occupation under the language of self-defence. All the while, these practices actually undermine both ceasefire agreements and established principles of international law, threatening Lebanon's sovereignty.

events.title: Robot beats human half-marathon world record
events.summary: #China's technological developments were on full display, and at full speed, in the robot half-marathon in #Beijing on April 19. The winning robot beat the human half-marathon record by nearly seven minutes, finishing in 50 minutes and 26 seconds.

events.title: US tariff refund system launches for companies to claim billions in import taxes
events.summary: The US Customs and Border Protection announced Monday that its refund portal for Trump's import tariffs – struck down by the US Supreme Court in mid-February – is now open to applications from businesses. The agency said in court filings that more than 330,000 importers paid a total of about $166 billion on over 53 million shipments.

events.title: Scientists, authorities divided over fate of stranded whale in Baltic Sea
events.summary: Timmy, a humpback whale, first became stuck off Germany's Baltic coast in late March. The animal’s plight has since gripped the public and raised questions about whether humans should intervene to save wild animals. While a rescue mission funded by two German millionaires is underway, marine biologists warn the whale has no chance of survival and are urging the public to reflect on humanity's role in the degradation of marine habitats. FRANCE 24's Environment Editor Valerie Dekimpe takes a closer look.

events.title: Ceasefire at risk after US seizes Iranian ship, Iran shuns peace talks
events.summary: A ceasefire between the United States and Iran appeared in jeopardy on Monday after the US said it had seized an Iranian cargo ship that tried to run its blockade and Tehran vowed to retaliate, refusing for now to join new peace talks. Iranian Foreign Ministry spokesman Esmaeil Baghaei said Washington has shown it was "not serious" about pursuing the diplomatic process ​and Tehran would not change ‌its clearly stated demands, adding that it did not believe in deadlines or ultimatums when safeguarding national interests. FRANCE 24's International Affairs Editor Angela Diffley tells us more.

events.title: Eight children killed in US domestic violence shooting
events.summary: A man shot dead eight children -- seven of them his own -- early Sunday in the southern US state of Louisiana in an incident of domestic violence that spanned three locations, police said. The early morning massacre at a gray house in the city of Shreveport was the deadliest mass shooting in the United States in more than two years, according to data from the Gun Violence Archive. Two women were shot and seriously injured, including the mother of some of the slain children.

events.title: 'Anatomy of a Cry': Crystal Murray on teen fame and going indie
events.summary: French-American artist Crystal Murray rose to fame on social media, a decade before the term influencer was "a thing". She was already making alternative R'n'B and soul, which led her to get signed and release a first album in 2024. A couple of years ago, she moved to London to find her voice on her own terms and became an independent artist. She popped by arts24 to tell Marjorie Hache about her new EP "Anatomy of a Cry", which sees her already avant-garde style tinted with indie folk. They also discuss new releases by Massive Attack, the Foo Fighters and Fally Ipupa.

events.title: Macron meets Tusk to discuss possible Polish role in French nuclear deterrence
events.summary: French President Emmanuel Macron and Polish Prime Minister Donald Tusk met Monday at a summit in the Polish city of Gdansk to discuss defence cooperation between the two countries. As Europe ramps up its defence capabilities amid growing threats from Russia and US President Donald Trump's questioning of NATO, both sides have said that Poland could contribute to French nuclear deterrence.

events.title: Philippines, US and allies start military exercises testing 'real‑world' readiness
events.summary: Philippine and US forces will carry out maritime strike drills on a remote Philippine island near Taiwan during annual exercises that started on Monday, which Manila's military chief said would ​test their readiness under "real-world ‌conditions". The April 20 to May 8 exercises known as "Balikatan", or "shoulder-to-shoulder", will be ⁠the largest yet in terms of participating countries, with Australia included again and Canada, France, New Zealand and Japan joining as active participants for the first ‌time.

events.title: Rebuilding Gaza will cost more than £50bn, says UN
events.summary: While the focus of many in the Middle East has been on conflicts in Lebanon and Iran, the scale of damage in Gaza is also back under scrutiny.

events.title: Migrants in Spain queue to apply for mass legalisation programme
events.summary: Migrants in Spain have queued up to apply for the country's mass legalisation programme on Monday.

events.title: IDF soldier damages Jesus statue with sledgehammer in Lebanon - as Netanyahu responds
events.summary: Israel's prime minister Benjamin Netanyahu condemned "in the strongest terms" images of an Israeli soldier damaging a statue of Jesus.

events.title: Ukraine police chief resigns after officers reportedly fled Kyiv shooting
events.summary: A police chief in Ukraine has resigned after officers reportedly fled the scene of a shooting in Kyiv which killed seven people.

events.title: Rebel Wilson appears in court for start of defamation trial over social media posts
events.summary: Rebel Wilson has appeared in court for the start of a defamation trial brought against her by another actress who starred in her directorial debut.

events.title: State of emergency declared in New Zealand after flash flooding
events.summary: A state of emergency has been declared in New Zealand after heavy rain triggered flash flooding.

events.title: Rat poison found in jars of baby food in Austria, police say
events.summary: A baby food brand is recalling its products sold in Austrian SPAR markets after some tested positive for rat poison.

events.title: At least six killed in Kyiv shooting
events.summary: At least six people have been killed after a gunman opened fire in Kyiv.

events.title: Drone attack kills 3 Colombian soldiers as rebel groups develop new lethal capabilities
events.summary: A Colombian rebel drone strike Monday killed three soldiers and injured two others, according to authorities

events.title: Gunman at pyramids north of Mexico City kills 1 Canadian tourist and injures 6
events.summary: Mexico's government says one Canadian tourist has been killed and six other people were injured when a man with a gun opened fire at the historic Teotihuacán pyramids, a tourist site an hour outside of Mexico’s capital

events.title: US family seeks justice in the death of daughter 7 years ago in Colombia
events.summary: Seven years after U.S. citizen Kelly Knight was found dead in Medellín, Colombia, her family seeks international legal help, claiming the Colombian justice system has failed them

events.title: Cuba confirms meeting with US officials on island, wants energy blockade lifted
events.summary: Cuba’s government is confirming that it recently met with U.S. officials on the island

events.title: US Embassy officials die in accident in Chihuahua; Mexican president wants answers
events.summary: President was unaware of any collaboration between U.S. and local authorities.

events.title: Slovaks to vote in a July referendum on lifelong payments for Prime Minister Fico and others
events.summary: Slovakia will hold a referendum this summer to decide on whether to cancel lifelong payments for Prime Minister Robert Fico and other leaders after their terms end

events.title: Hungary's Magyar announces ministers after landslide election win
events.summary: Hungarian election winner Péter Magyar is announcing the first round of his incoming cabinet members following his party's landslide victory over Prime Minister Viktor Orbán

events.title: EU says Serbia could lose access to a billion euros over democratic backsliding
events.summary: The EU is warning Serbia it could lose access to 1.5 billion euros in funds if it doesn't halt democratic backsliding

events.title: A top paramilitary commander defects to Sudan’s military as war enters 4th year
events.summary: A high-profile paramilitary commander in Sudan has switched sides and joined the country’s army as the war enters a fourth year

events.title: Migrants rush to apply under Spain’s new mass legalization program
events.summary: Spain has launched a mass legalization measure for migrants, allowing them to apply for legal status starting on Monday

events.title: Bus accident in Indian-controlled Kashmir kills 21 people
events.summary: A passenger bus has slid off a Himalayan highway in Indian-controlled Kashmir, killing at least 21 people and injuring about 45 others

events.title: What to know about Timmy, the humpback whale that's sick and stranded in Baltic Sea
events.summary: A humpback whale’s likely final days in the Baltic Sea have been livestreamed across the globe as multiple rescue efforts failed to coax it back into deeper waters while the marine mammal gets sicker and weaker

events.title: Rat poison found in some baby food jars in Central Europe leads to recall
events.summary: Countries across Central Europe have pulled HiPP baby food off shelves after rat poison was found in some jars over the weekend

events.title: What to know as ceasefire in the Iran war hangs in the balance
events.summary: The ceasefire in the Iran war hangs in the balance as Pakistan’s capital stands prepared for possible new talks between Tehran and Washington

events.title: Tired of political turmoil, Bulgarians give ex-president a convincing mandate for change
events.summary: Bulgaria's center-left coalition led by ex-president Rumen Radev has won the parliamentary election, according to the central electoral commission

events.title: India and South Korea agree to nearly double trade to $50B by 2030
events.summary: South Korea and India have agreed to nearly double bilateral trade to $50 billion by 2030, up from about $27 billion

events.title: Japan warns of slightly increased risk of mega-quake after a 7.7-magnitude one
events.summary: Japan has issued an advisory for northern coastal areas for an increased risk of a possible mega-quake following a 7.7-magnitude quake earlier Monday

events.title: SECURITY COUNCIL LIVE: Emergency briefing on Ukraine amid escalating Russian attacks

The open briefing follows a formal request from Kyiv citing a surge in Russian aerial bombardments, including devastating strikes on the city of Dnipro. Ukraine has reported that between late March and mid-April, more than 5,000 drones and missiles were launched, killing dozens of civilians and injuring hundreds more. Follow full meetings coverage below and UN News app users can follow here.

A UN peacekeeper was also killed over the weekend – likely from Hezbollah fire.  Stay with us for live updates. App users can follow coverage here.

He also welcomed Iran’s announcement that the Strait of Hormuz will remain open to commercial vessels for the remainder of the ceasefire, calling it a step in the right direction. Stay with us for live updates. App users can follow coverage here.
